A deep 2-4 hour demo will follow. Anthony Gray is fronting from Zycus side. The named in-house agentic-AI experimentation indicates technical capability and openness on the buyer side.

sources: Avoma discovery call 9 Apr 2026, Mariano (is_rep: false)
metrics.narrative: Mariano articulated operational pain on the 9 Apr discovery call: Ahlstrom is running on legacy SAP Ariba (since 2022) which lacks AI capabilities, has poor user experience, and is not meeting their needs for supplier lifecycle management, risk management, and contract management. He stated the organization wants to 'break away where it makes sense' from the broader SAP footprint (S/4HANA, SuccessFactors) to gain modern S2P functionality. The scope is full S2P: upstream (sourcing, contracting, SRM, risk—top priority) and downstream P2P (eInvoicing, eProcurement) where feasible given S/4HANA integration constraints. However, no quantified business case has been shared: no cost of the current pain (cycle time, maverick spend, supplier risk incidents, manual effort), no target ROI, no savings target, no headcount impact. Mariano did not provide a dollar or percentage metric. The pain is qualitative only. Until a quantified business case is built, securing executive and PE-owner buy-in for a 2027 capital investment will be difficult. Quantifying the value case is a critical next move.

champion.narrative: Mariano (head of procurement excellence function, last name not provided on call) is the only champion candidate, and he is weak and at high risk. On the 9 Apr discovery call he advocated for Zycus by articulating the pain with Ariba, expressing interest in AI capabilities (Merlin, agentic AI), and committing to launch the RFI within 1-2 weeks. However, his engagement has been passive since that call: the RFI never launched, the NDA was never executed, the demo was never scheduled, and he has not responded to the rep's 25 Jun follow-up. He has not introduced Zycus to the Economic Buyer, Decision Maker, or C-level sponsor (none identified), and he has not multi-threaded the relationship (Jinxia, the named RFI lead, has never engaged despite being listed as an OpportunityContactRole Evaluator). Mariano has no apparent budget authority and stated the project is 'speculative' pending PE exit and new-owner funding. His role is excellence/process, not commercial decision-making. He has not demonstrated ability to drive the process, navigate internal politics (finance/IT influence on P2P), or secure executive buy-in. After 11 weeks of silence, the risk is high that the project has been deprioritized or shelved pending the PE exit outcome, and Mariano may have disengaged. A weak, cooling, at-risk champion with no executive access and no multi-threading is a critical vulnerability. Developing a stronger champion (Jinxia or a finance/IT leader) and securing an executive sponsor are urgent moves.

competition.narrative: Competition is confirmed across Salesforce fields and the discovery call. The Competitors__c field (Q3) lists 'Coupa;GEP Worldwide;SAP Ariba'. Mariano confirmed on 9 Apr that the RFI will go to Zycus, Ariba (incumbent), Coupa, and GEP. SAP Ariba is the primary competitive threat: it has been the incumbent since 2022, is embedded in a broader SAP enterprise agreement (S/4HANA, SuccessFactors), and benefits from SAP's relationship leverage and integration lock-in. Mariano acknowledged Ahlstrom is 'tied to SAP' and expressed desire to 'break away where it makes sense,' but also noted that finance and IT have 'significant influence' on downstream P2P tooling due to S/4HANA dependencies, which creates political and technical barriers to displacing Ariba. The buyer is running a competitive RFI, so Coupa and GEP are live threats, but Ariba's incumbency, contract lock-in, and internal political influence make it the toughest competitor. The wedge to win: position Zycus as the AI-first, upstream-specialist alternative that delivers the modern SRM, risk, and contract capabilities Ariba lacks, with Merlin as the differentiation anchor. Emphasis on upstream (where Ariba is weakest and finance/IT influence is lowest) and on AI/agentic capabilities (which the buyer explicitly prioritized) is the path to displacing Ariba. Downstream P2P can be phased or co-exist with S/4HANA if needed. The how-we-win: lead with upstream AI innovation, quantify the gap vs Ariba on SRM/risk/contract, engage finance/IT early to design the integration, and escalate to an executive sponsor who can override the inertia of the SAP relationship.

For an Oct close, RFI should have launched in April, shortlist by late May, demos/workshops June-July, commercials Aug, and contracting Sept. Pace required: to close Oct 2026, the buyer would need to launch RFI, complete vendor evaluation, select Zycus, secure executive approval and budget (which does not exist), negotiate, and contract in 114 days—impossible given 11 weeks of inactivity and the stated 2027 implementation timeline.

flags: Budget and EB mapping are the single largest risk. Project is pre-budget, speculative, contingent on PE exit and 2027 capital injection; no CFO, CPO, or C-level sponsor identified or engaged. Without an EB and a committed budget, this RFI may complete but never convert to a funded RFP. Immediate move: map the approval chain (who will sponsor the business case to the PE owner or post-exit C-suite, who controls 2027 budget) and secure an executive stakeholder meeting (ideally with the relevant stakeholder or the finance lead) to validate the project's strategic priority and financial backing. Co-build a quantified ROI case (savings, cycle-time, AI efficiency) in a half-day workshop to give the buyer a board-ready business case.

Relationship has gone quiet for 11 weeks; champion is weak and at risk. Mariano was engaged and positive on 9 Apr, but has not followed through on any commitment (no RFI, no demo, no NDA, no reply to 25 Jun follow-up). Tiziana committed to fast-track RFI launch 'next week,' now 11 weeks overdue. Jinxia (named as RFI lead) has never engaged. This is a cooling, single-threaded deal with no executive sponsorship. Immediate move: re-engage Mariano with a specific value hook (1-hour AI/Merlin executive briefing for his leadership, or commit to the half-day workshop to unblock the RFI) within 3 business days. If no reply in 7 days, escalate through Anthony Gray (owner's manager, was on 9 Apr call, known to Mariano) to reopen the relationship at a senior level and assess whether the project is still live or shelved pending PE exit outcome.

AI differentiation is the wedge; make Merlin the hero. Mariano validated the agentic AI vision on 9 Apr ('you get that it changes the entire workflow, not just automates tasks… a lot of what I'm seeing is just automating tasks, not eliminating workflows'). This is Zycus's strongest competitive advantage vs. Ariba (no agentic capability), Coupa (task automation only), and GEP (analytics-focused). Activation: lead the 2-4 hour demo (when scheduled) with the 3 agentic workflows (touchless invoicing, tail-spend negotiation, supplier onboarding) in the first 20 minutes, so the project team sees the transformation immediately. Make Merlin control tower (Teams-native intake, conversational interface) the second hero — show how it eliminates the fragmented email/phone/Excel channels Mariano described as pain. In the RFI response, anchor every answer to AI capability and cite 2-3 European customers who have eliminated workflows (not just improved cycle times) with Zycus agentic AI.

competitive_position.summary: SAP Ariba is the entrenched incumbent (since 2022) with contract lock-in, SAP enterprise-agreement leverage, and finance/IT political support for downstream P2P. Coupa and GEP are RFI participants but secondary threats. Zycus is differentiated on upstream AI capabilities (Merlin, agentic AI) and modern SRM/risk/contract functionality, but has not yet engaged the buyer to prove it. The RFI has not launched, so competitive positioning has not begun. High risk that Ariba wins by inertia if Zycus cannot catalyze the evaluation and secure an executive sponsor.

hard.ais_why:
• Apr 2026 Avoma call captures Ahlstrom's Mariano explicitly stating they 'now seek a next-generation AI-driven Source-to-Pay solution' to replace SAP Ariba. The procurement team is experimenting with agentic AI tools in-house and Mariano recognised that rapid AI advances over the last 6 months drove the decision to launch this evaluation. This is verbatim buyer-side AI-led displacement framing ‚Äî Mariano named both the goal (AI-driven S2P) and the catalyst (last 6 months of AI advances).
• Strong AI product list (Agentic AI, Merlin, plus full S2P stack) at $400K with BD_Engagement Engaged. An RFI is launching within 2 weeks with NDA signature requested. A deep 2-4 hour demo will follow. Anthony Gray is fronting from Zycus side. The named in-house agentic-AI experimentation indicates technical capability and openness on the buyer side.
• High confidence: the call documents AI as the explicit RFI driver. Held at 8 (not 9) because the implementation timeline is contingent on a PE exit / capital injection in 2027 ‚Äî financial event risk could delay commitment. A signed RFI-with-AI-mandate would push to 9.

Finance and IT organizations have 'significant influence' in tool selection for P2P, and their alignment is required but not yet secured. Risk that finance/IT block a move away from Ariba, or that the SAP contract renewal/exit terms make switching economically unfeasible.

value.flag: AI differentiation is the wedge; make Merlin the hero. Mariano validated the agentic AI vision on 9 Apr ('you get that it changes the entire workflow, not just automates tasks… a lot of what I'm seeing is just automating tasks, not eliminating workflows'). This is Zycus's strongest competitive advantage vs. Ariba (no agentic capability), Coupa (task automation only), and GEP (analytics-focused). Activation: lead the 2-4 hour demo (when scheduled) with the 3 agentic workflows (touchless invoicing, tail-spend negotiation, supplier onboarding) in the first 20 minutes, so the project team sees the transformation immediately. Make Merlin control tower (Teams-native intake, conversational interface) the second hero — show how it eliminates the fragmented email/phone/Excel channels Mariano described as pain. In the RFI response, anchor every answer to AI capability and cite 2-3 European customers who have eliminated workflows (not just improved cycle times) with Zycus agentic AI.
